content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : File - 82015                                    Date : Aug. 3, 1978




From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
        Barbara Campbell

Subject: Advice on newspaper advertising discount


Kirk Pessner of Dobbs and Nielsen called today with the
following situation. A newspaper has regular rates for commercial
and political advertisers. Those rates are lower than the rates
available to the general public. The political rates are given
to all political advertisers. The commercial and political rates
are scheduled for an increase, effective October 1. Kirk asked
if the newspaper retains the lower political advertising rate
until after the election, will the newspaper be making in-kind
contributions (in the form of a discount) to its political
advertisers?

I advised Kirk that the above-described situation is not a
contribution.

I based my advice on that portion of Government Code Section
82015 which defines the "granting of discounts ... by ... news-
papers not extended on an equal basis to all candidates for the
same office" as a contribution.

In the situation Pessner described, the lower political ad-
vertising rate is available to all political advertisers and is
therefore not a contribution under the Political Reform Act.

BC:plh
cc: Ted

sections.question: If the newspaper retains the lower political advertising rate until after the election, will the newspaper be making in-kind contributions (in the form of a discount) to its political advertisers?
sections.conclusion: The above-described situation is not a contribution. The lower political advertising rate is available to all political advertisers and is therefore not a contribution under the Political Reform Act.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a newspaper's decision to maintain lower political advertising rates through an election date constitutes an in-kind contribution to political advertisers under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The newspaper's offer of lower political advertising rates available equally to all political advertisers does not constitute an in-kind contribution, as it is not extended on an unequal basis to different candidates.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Memorandum format rather than formal advice letter; no formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION section headers; legal basis cited as Government Code Section 82015; document dated August 3, 1978

embedding.summary: FPPC staff advises that a newspaper's maintenance of lower political advertising rates available equally to all political advertisers does not constitute an in-kind contribution under Government Code Section 82015.
